title: lapsana communis subsp. adenophora
intro: Lapsana communis subsp. adenophora, also known as the bitter dodder, is a subspecies of the Lapsana communis plant that belongs to the Compositae family of flowering plants. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems. The health benefits of Lapsana communis subsp. adenophora include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
